taxonomy: Tragacantha meschhedensis is classified as a species of shrub within the Fabaceae family, commonly known as legumes. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Tragacantha meschhedensis is further grouped into the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of plants such as beans, peas, and vetches. It is also assigned to the genus Tragacantha, which is a group of plants that are often referred to as hair-grasses or milk-vetches. The classification of Tragacantha meschhedensis provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and its relationship with other plants.

intro: Tragacantha meschhedensis, also known as Astragalus meschhedensis, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, and research has identified several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities that may aid in boosting the immune system and reducing the severity of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha meschhedensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory infections, and to support the immune system.
